_comment: Built-in defaults for capability → concrete skill mapping AND model role → model alias (sonnet/opus/haiku). Override at: ~/.flow/config.yaml (user global) → <project>/.flow/config.yaml (project) → <project>/.flow/config.local.yaml (project local). install-time render substitutes {{capability:X}} and {{model:X}} placeholders in prompt files (claude/{commands,skills}/flow/*.md). model_roles.*.default holds the alias (Agent tool's `model` param is enum-restricted) — concrete IDs (and 1M-context variants) are picked at runtime via ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL env vars in ~/.claude/settings.json. Per-role `fallback` field documents the retry alias when primary dispatch fails (consumed by start.md / SKILL.md protocol text, not by resolve_model itself).

tdd.description: Phase 2 — write tests first; gate before implementation

worktree.description: Phase 2 — isolated worktree per task or sub-agent

cross_model_challenge.description: Phase 2 — adversarial cross-model attack when stuck

cross_model_review.description: Phase 3 — cross-model diff review at task close

deploy_chain.description: Phase 2/3 (deploy task) — ship → canary monitoring chain

behavioral_guidelines.description: Phase 2 — surgical changes / define success criteria / surface assumptions / avoid over-engineering

multi_step_plan.description: Phase 1 — Manus-style file-protocol planning for B/C-size tasks (multi-day, multi-file)

dev_setup.description: Phase 1 (deploy task) — detect platform (Fly/Render/Vercel/Netlify/Heroku/GitHub Actions/custom) + write deploy config

subagent_discipline.description: Phase 2 — sub-agent prompts + return contracts; pairs with parallel_dispatch (the latter handles orchestration mechanics)

execute_plan_discipline.description: Phase 2 — discipline for following written implementation plans (closes the loop with multi_step_plan)

systematic_debug.description: Phase 2 — iron-law 4-phase root-cause discipline; first-line debug before reaching for fixes

deep_investigate.description: Phase 2 — heavier debugging pipeline; escalation path when systematic_debug isn't enough

land_and_deploy.description: Phase 2 (deploy task) — one-shot merge + deploy + canary verify; alternative to deploy_chain (for small confident changes; deploy_chain is for large features needing separate ship → wait → land)

verify_completion.description: Phase 3 — MANDATORY at entry; runs verification commands and confirms output before any 'done' claim. Closes a security-class gap where Flow previously allowed self-reported success without actual verification

code_review_small.description: Phase 3 — daily-driver reviewer (5 Sonnet parallel + Haiku confidence scoring) for diff < 200 lines, single module

code_review_large.description: Phase 3 — 6-specialist agent panel for diff ≥ 200 lines or multi-module PRs (high coverage but token-heavy)

review_request_etiquette.description: Phase 3 — pair: superpowers:requesting-code-review BEFORE invoking code_review_small/large (clear scope, what to look at); superpowers:receiving-code-review AFTER reviewer responds (verify-before-agreeing, don't perform-agree). The comma-separated value is opaque to the resolver; SKILL.md prompt must reference each half at the correct point in the workflow.

pre_land_review.description: Phase 3 — pre-merge diff review for SQL safety / LLM trust boundaries / conditional side effects; specialist patterns the general reviewers miss

quality_health.description: Phase 3 — composite 0-10 quality score using existing project tools (typecheck + lint + tests + dead code); fast quality gate at Phase 3 entry

perf_baseline.description: Phase 3 — Web Vitals + resource size baseline regression compare for perf-sensitive backend tasks; pairs with quality_health (one for code quality, the other for runtime performance)

post_deploy_qa.description: Phase 3 (deploy task) — active QA on deployed site (clicks login, fills forms); complements canary's passive monitoring (canary alerts on failure; post_deploy_qa actively verifies success)

branch_finish.description: Phase 4 — structured options for merge / PR / cleanup; closes the loop after sediment is written

changelog_gen.description: Phase 4 — auto-generate user-facing changelog from commit history; eliminates manual CHANGELOG.md editing step (validate output once before flipping default; may overwrite vs append)

capabilities._v0_6_cross_cutting_additions: Cross-cutting capabilities added in v0.6.0 (any phase)

safety_guardrails.description: Cross-cutting — destructive command warnings (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push, git reset --hard, kubectl delete, migrations); orchestrator invokes before any destructive op. Hook-based auto-fire deferred to v0.7

weekly_retro.description: Cross-cutting — cross-task weekly review (commits / work patterns / quality trends). User-triggered or scheduled via /loop weekly. Fills the per-task-vs-cross-task gap (Phase 4 sediment is per-task; retro is per-week)

capabilities._v0_7_phase2_additions: Phase 2 capabilities added in v0.7.0 (wave dispatch)

wave_planning.description: Phase 2 — decompose plan into waves (mechanical disjointness + LLM concept-veto + cache)

wave_dispatch.description: Phase 2 — execute one wave: paired implementer/spec-reviewer in parallel, sequential merge with FILES_CHANGED verification, code-quality reviewer at wave end

capabilities._v0_8_phase2_additions: Phase 2 capabilities added in v0.8.0 (autonomy orchestrator)

autonomy_orchestrator.description: Phase 2 — autonomous orchestrator (v0.8.1 promoted: worktree-per-task dispatch + 8-gate runner + Notifier + crash recovery). v0.8.1 ships dispatch SHIM infrastructure only; production subagent invocation requires operator to export FLOW_SUBAGENT_DISPATCH_CMD env var (no built-in default — the SKILL handle is not a shell command and would fail rc=127 if executed). Production wire-up of the Claude CLI invocation template is v0.8.2 scope.
autonomy_orchestrator.dispatch_cmd_note: Intentionally absent — must be supplied via FLOW_SUBAGENT_DISPATCH_CMD env var. Template placeholders: {slug}, {task_id} (validated alphanumeric+`._+-`); {worktree} (raw path, backward-compatible with templates wrapping in their own shell quotes) and {worktree_quoted} (shlex.quote()-wrapped, RECOMMENDED for shell=True). Example: `claude --skill flow-phase2-execute --slug {slug} --task {task_id} --worktree {worktree_quoted}`. See scripts/flow_subagent_dispatch.py::_resolve_cmd_template fallback message.

acceptance_verify.description: Phase 3 — acceptance_criteria runner gate (v0.8.1 promoted: all 5 criterion-type executors + Phase 3 retry routing)

triage.description: Cheap classification (task complexity, keyword detection). Alias resolves via ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL env var.

research.description: Sub-agent research / scanning / cross-file inspection. Alias resolves via ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL (1M variant recommended). On dispatch failure (model unavailable), retry with `opus` — never downgrade to `haiku`.

plan.description: Plan writing + ADR drafting. Alias resolves via env var. On failure, fall back to opus.

implement.description: Code generation / refactoring / debugging. Alias resolves via ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL env var.

review.description: Self-review of diffs (when cross_model_review skipped). Alias resolves via env var. On failure, fall back to opus.
